summary: This is a very brief announcement post where Joel Spolsky shares two pieces of news: a comment from his publisher Gary Cornell about the cover of the 'Joel on Software' book, and an announcement about a new crowd-sourced book project tentatively titled '// comment'. The book would collect the 30 best essays about computer software from the past year, as nominated and voted on by Joel on Software readers. Joel invites readers to participate in the nomination and voting process.
key_points: Joel's publisher Gary Cornell commented on the book cover of 'Joel on Software' generating questions from readers
Joel announced a new book project tentatively titled '// comment' that would be a curated collection of the best software essays
The book concept was community-driven, with readers nominating and voting on which essays to include
money_quotes: It's going to be your book: the 30 best essays about computer software over the last year… the kinds of things that Joel on Software readers want to read, edited and introduced by me.
key_insight: Joel leveraged his engaged reader community to crowdsource content for a curated anthology of the best software writing.
